title: Implement feedback invalidate CLI and MCP tool
description:
Implement the single-item feedback invalidation workflow described in plx-f21daa, including deterministic identifier resolution, GraphQL mutation plumbing, CLI registration, MCP registration, and automated tests.

Notes:
- This tool is for operator-approved targeted invalidation only.
- It must not batch invalidate or silently choose among multiple candidate feedback items.
- Prefer shared resolution logic between CLI and MCP to keep behavior identical.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Started implementation. Current design: accept a single identifier, resolve it first as a feedback item ID, then as an item ID / identifier-table value / externalId. If resolution lands on an item with multiple feedback items, require explicit --scorecard and --score disambiguation instead of mutating multiple records. CLI and MCP will share the same resolver/mutation helper.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Implemented the single-item invalidation workflow.

What changed:
- Added a shared feedback invalidation helper that resolves a target as direct feedback ID first, then item ID / identifier-table value / externalId.
- Added `FeedbackItem.invalidate()` plus `isInvalid` in update mutation responses.
- Added `plexus feedback invalidate <identifier>` with optional `--scorecard` and `--score` disambiguation.
- Added MCP tool `plexus_feedback_invalidate` with the same single-item semantics and structured ambiguity errors.
- Extended shared item resolution to support item externalId lookups.

Verification so far:
- Focused unit tests: 13 passed.
- Adjacent feedback/MCP tests: 42 passed.
- CLI import smoke test for `plexus.cli.feedback.commands`: passed.
- Editable CLI help confirms `plexus feedback invalidate --help` is live.
